close_reason: Implemented config.py with load/write, mm init command, removed all hardcoded user-specific strings. 234 tests pass, lint clean.
description:
## Problem

mm-cli has two hardcoded user-specific strings that prevent other users from using the tool:

1. **`TRANSFER_CATEGORY_ROOT = "Umbuchungen"`** in `analysis.py:20` — used to detect transfer categories by path prefix
2. **`"aufgelöst"`** in `cli.py:122` — used to filter out dissolved account groups

MoneyMoney's AppleScript API does NOT expose:
- A `type` field on categories (no way to distinguish transfer vs income/expense)
- A dissolved/archived flag on accounts (the UI checkbox 'Das Konto wurde aufgelöst' is not in the export)

These must be user-configured.

## Solution

Add a config file at `~/.config/mm-cli/config.toml`:

```toml
transfer_category = "Umbuchungen"
excluded_groups = ["Aufgelöst"]
```

- Defaults should be empty (no filtering) so the tool works out of the box for any user
- Replace hardcoded references in `analysis.py` and `cli.py` with config lookups
- Consider adding an `mm init` command that helps users configure by showing their actual MM categories/groups

## Investigation notes

- Tested against real MoneyMoney data (2026-01-27)
- Category export has keys: budget, currency, default, group, indentation, name, rules, uuid — NO type field
- Account export has keys: accountNumber, attributes, balance, bankCode, currency, group, indentation, name, owner, portfolio, refreshTimestamp, type, uuid — NO dissolved flag
- 'Umbuchungen' may be a default MM category (needs verification) — if so, it could be a sensible default
- EUR currency fallback in applescript.py is also potentially user-specific

## Files to modify

- `mm_cli/analysis.py` — remove `TRANSFER_CATEGORY_ROOT` hardcoding
- `mm_cli/cli.py` — remove `aufgelöst` hardcoding, add `mm init` command
- `mm_cli/config.py` — new file for config loading
- `pyproject.toml` — add `tomli` dependency if needed
title: Add user config file to make mm-cli user-agnostic

close_reason: Using azad Chrome extension for order export. Custom scraper documented in skill for reference. Future work: integrate with second-brain purchase command and mm CLI.
description:
## Goal
Extract all Amazon orders from 2024-01-01 to present and enrich with product category info for MoneyMoney categorization.

## Workflow (Two-Step Approach)

### Step 1: Extract Orders (Fast)
1. Navigate to Amazon orders page
2. Set date range (2024, 2025, 2026)
3. Run extraction script per page
4. Paginate through all pages
5. Save raw order data with ASINs to JSON/CSV

### Step 2: Enrich with Categories (Haiku Model)
For each unique ASIN, use WebSearch with haiku model:
- Search: "Amazon ASIN {asin} product category"
- Extract: Product name, category from search results
- Map Amazon category to MoneyMoney category

## Implementation Notes
- Use haiku model for ASIN lookups (cost-effective for many requests)
- Batch ASIN lookups to avoid rate limits
- Cache ASIN→category mappings (same product = same category)

## Output
CSV: order_date, order_id, total_eur, item_name, item_asin, amazon_category, mm_category

## Integration
- Match to MoneyMoney transactions by date/amount
- Export to second-brain for purchase tracking (see related task)
notes:
## Update 2026-01-29

Custom browser scraper approach didn't work reliably. Using azad Chrome extension instead:
- Install from: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/amazon-order-history-repo/mgkilgclilajckgnedgjgnfdokkgnibi
- Click year buttons to export orders to CSV

The amazon-orders skill documents the extraction patterns from azad for reference.

Future integration: tie Amazon order data into second-brain `purchase` command, potentially with `mm` CLI calls for category matching.
title: Extract Amazon orders since 2024-01-01 using amazon-orders skill

close_reason: Implemented IBAN-based transfer detection. counterparty_iban field added to Transaction, filter_transfers rewritten with IBAN+group logic, all 5 analyze commands wired up. 7 new tests, live tested.
description:
Currently, transfers are detected by checking if a transaction's category falls under the Umbuchungen category tree. This works because MoneyMoney auto-categorizes same-group (same legal entity) transfers as 'Echte Umbuchung' and cross-group transfers get proper income/expense categories (e.g. Gehalt).

However, a structural IBAN-based approach would be more robust: detect transfers by checking if the counterparty's IBAN matches one of the user's own accounts.

Key concept: group = legal entity.
- Same-group transfer (e.g. Privat→Privat): internal shuffle, exclude
- Cross-group transfer (e.g. cognovis→Privat salary): real cashflow, keep

Implementation:
1. Add counterparty_iban field to Transaction model (from plist accountNumber field)
2. Build own-IBAN set from accounts
3. Match counterparty IBAN against own accounts
4. Same-group → exclude, cross-group → keep
5. Keep category-based detection as fallback (credit card settlements lack counterparty IBAN)

This also serves as validation for correct Umbuchungen categorization and catches uncategorized transfers.
title: IBAN-based transfer detection for analysis commands

4. analysis.py (NEU): resolve_period(), compute_spending() Logik.
5. output.py: output_spending() mit Budget-Spalten, Compare-Spalte, Farbcodierung.

Perioden: this-month, last-month, this-quarter, last-quarter, this-year. Auch --from/--to moeglich.
Budget-Periodenumrechnung (Monat->Quartal etc.) als Folge-Ticket.

title: Category spending analysis: budget vs actual, period comparison

close_reason: Split into specific beads: mm-cli-0kt (portfolio), mm-cli-t41 (checkmark/comment), mm-cli-qpf (transfer). Direct debit, batch operations, and offline accounts dropped as not needed.
description:
## Overview
MoneyMoney provides extensive AppleScript automation beyond basic data retrieval. This ticket covers exposing all additional capabilities through the CLI.

## Reference
Official documentation: https://moneymoney.app/applescript/

## Additional Commands to Implement

### Portfolio Management
#### `mm portfolio`
Export securities holdings.
- AppleScript: `export portfolio from account ... as "plist"`
- Options:
  - `--account` / `-a`: Specific account (optional, all if omitted)
- Output: Security name, ISIN, quantity, value, performance

### Bank Transfers
#### `mm transfer`
Create a bank transfer.
- AppleScript: `create bank transfer from account ... to ... iban ... amount ... purpose ...`
- Arguments:
  - `--from-account`: Source account
  - `--to`: Recipient name
  - `--iban`: Recipient IBAN
  - `--amount`: Transfer amount
  - `--purpose`: Transfer purpose/reference
- Options:
  - `--outbox`: Save to outbox without opening UI
  - `--dry-run`: Show transfer details without creating

#### `mm batch-transfer`
Import SEPA-XML batch transfer.
- AppleScript: `create batch transfer from POSIX file ...`
- Arguments:
  - `file`: Path to SEPA-XML file
- Options:
  - `--validate`: Validate XML without importing

### Direct Debits
#### `mm direct-debit`
Create a direct debit.
- AppleScript: `create direct debit from account ... for ... iban ... amount ... purpose ... mandate reference ... mandate date ... scheduled date ...`
- Arguments:
  - `--from-account`: Creditor account
  - `--for`: Debtor name
  - `--iban`: Debtor IBAN
  - `--amount`: Debit amount
  - `--purpose`: Purpose text
  - `--mandate-ref`: SEPA mandate reference
  - `--mandate-date`: Mandate signature date (YYYY-MM-DD)
  - `--scheduled-date`: Execution date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Options:
  - `--outbox`: Save to outbox without opening UI

#### `mm batch-direct-debit`
Import SEPA-XML batch direct debit.
- AppleScript: `create batch direct debit from POSIX file ...`
- Arguments:
  - `file`: Path to SEPA-XML file

### Offline Account Management
#### `mm add-transaction`
Add transaction to offline account.
- AppleScript: `add transaction to account ... on date ... to ... amount ... purpose ... category ...`
- Arguments:
  - `--account`: Target offline account name
  - `--date`: Transaction date (YYYY-MM-DD)
  - `--to`: Payee/recipient name
  - `--amount`: Transaction amount (negative for expenses)
  - `--purpose`: Description/purpose
  - `--category`: Category name (optional)

### Transaction Management
#### `mm set-checkmark`
Set/unset transaction checkmark.
- AppleScript: `set transaction id [ID] checkmark to "on"/"off"`
- Arguments:
  - `transaction_id`: Transaction ID
  - `state`: on/off

#### `mm set-comment`
Add/update transaction note.
- AppleScript: `set transaction id [ID] comment to ...`
- Arguments:
  - `transaction_id`: Transaction ID
  - `comment`: Note text

### Export Enhancements
#### `mm export`
Unified export command with format options.
- Subcommands:
  - `mm export transactions --format csv/plist/json`
  - `mm export accounts --format plist/json`
  - `mm export categories --format plist/json`
  - `mm export portfolio --format plist/json`

## Implementation Notes

### Safety Considerations
- Transfer and direct debit commands should require `--confirm` flag or interactive confirmation
- Add `--dry-run` to all write operations
- Validate IBAN format before sending to AppleScript
- Validate dates and amounts

### Command Groups
Organize commands into logical groups:
- `mm accounts` - Account operations
- `mm transactions` - Transaction queries
- `mm categories` - Category management
- `mm transfers` - Transfer operations (transfer, batch-transfer)
- `mm debits` - Direct debit operations (direct-debit, batch-direct-debit)
- `mm portfolio` - Securities/portfolio

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All additional AppleScript commands exposed via CLI
- [ ] Safety confirmations for write operations
- [ ] IBAN validation
- [ ] Date format validation
- [ ] SEPA-XML validation for batch imports
- [ ] Comprehensive --help for all commands
- [ ] Integration tests with MoneyMoney (manual verification)
title: Expose additional MoneyMoney AppleScript functionality

close_reason: Closed
description:
## Problem

`mm transactions` without `--from`/`--to` fails with AppleScript error: 'Einige Parameter fehlen für export transactions' (-1701). MoneyMoney requires a date range but the CLI has no default.

This also affects `--group` and other filters when used without explicit dates.

## Solution

1. Add a `--days` / `-d` option (integer, default 14) that sets the date range to 'today minus N days'
2. When neither `--from`/`--to` nor `--days` is provided, default to last 14 days
3. `--days` should be mutually exclusive with `--from`/`--to` (or: `--days` sets `--from` if not explicitly provided)
4. Update help text to explain the default behavior

## Discovered during
Manual testing of mm-cli-gk5 (mm-cli-8oy group filter tests)
title: Add --days option and default date range for transactions command

close_reason: Closed
description:
## Problem
The mm categories command returns all 106 categories as flat/root-level items with no parent information. MoneyMoney's export categories AppleScript returns a flat plist. However, transaction data contains full category paths with backslash separators (e.g. Malte\Ausgaben\Leben\Ernährung), proving the hierarchy exists.

## Root Cause  
MoneyMoney's export categories plist has no children arrays. The _parse_category_tree() in applescript.py:228 handles children but there are none.

## Solution
Reconstruct hierarchy from transaction category paths. Export transactions, collect unique category paths, build tree from backslash-separated paths. Map category UUIDs via categoryUuid field.

## Impact
- 106 categories shown flat with no hierarchy
- Duplicate names (3x Sonstiges, 3x Nebenkosten, 2x Ausgaben) are indistinguishable
- Cannot suggest proper categories for uncategorized transactions
title: Fix category hierarchy - categories export is flat, losing parent-child structure

close_reason: Closed
description:
## Problem

`mm analyze balance-history` renders an unreadable table — columns appear as single-character-wide empty cells. The data is present (JSON output works fine), but the table rendering is broken.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `mm analyze balance-history` — garbled table
2. Run `mm analyze balance-history --format json` — works correctly
3. Run `mm analyze balance-history --account Girokonto` — table renders fine for single account

The bug appears when displaying multiple accounts — the table tries to create too many columns or the layout logic doesn't handle the multi-account case properly.

## Expected
Readable table with columns for each month and rows per account (or similar layout).

## Discovered during
Manual testing of mm-cli-gk5 (mm-cli-ty3 balance history tests)
title: Balance history table output is garbled/unreadable

close_reason: All 5 CLI commands implemented with full test coverage
description:
## Overview
Implement the core CLI commands to interact with MoneyMoney macOS app via AppleScript. This is the foundation for all MoneyMoney data access.

## Commands to Implement

### 1. `mm accounts`
List all accounts from MoneyMoney database.
- AppleScript: `export accounts`
- Returns: Account ID, name, balance, type, bank name
- Output formats: table (default), json

### 2. `mm transactions`
List transactions with filtering options.
- AppleScript: `export transactions from account ... from date ... to date ... as "plist"`
- Options:
  - `--account` / `-a`: Filter by account ID or name
  - `--from` / `-f`: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
  - `--to` / `-t`: End date (YYYY-MM-DD)  
  - `--category` / `-c`: Filter by category
  - `--uncategorized`: Show only uncategorized transactions
- Output formats: table (default), json, csv

### 3. `mm categories`
List all categories from MoneyMoney.
- AppleScript: `export categories`
- Returns: Category UUID, name, parent category, type (income/expense)
- Output formats: table (default), json

### 4. `mm category-usage`
Show categories sorted by transaction count.
- Requires: transactions export + category mapping
- Output: Category name, transaction count, total amount
- Options:
  - `--from` / `-f`: Start date
  - `--to` / `-t`: End date
  - `--limit` / `-n`: Top N categories

### 5. `mm set-category`
Update the category of a transaction.
- AppleScript: `set transaction id [ID] category to [UUID]`
- Arguments:
  - `transaction_id`: Transaction ID (from plist export)
  - `category`: Category name or UUID
- Options:
  - `--dry-run`: Show what would be changed without applying

## Technical Implementation

### AppleScript Module (`mm_cli/applescript.py`)
- `run_applescript(script: str) -> str`: Execute AppleScript via osascript
- `export_accounts() -> list[dict]`: Get all accounts
- `export_transactions(...) -> list[dict]`: Get transactions with filters
- `export_categories() -> list[dict]`: Get all categories
- `set_transaction_category(tx_id: str, category_id: str) -> bool`: Update category

### Models (`mm_cli/models.py`)
- `Account`: dataclass for account data
- `Transaction`: dataclass for transaction data
- `Category`: dataclass for category data

### Output Formatting (`mm_cli/output.py`)
- Rich tables for terminal output
- JSON serialization
- CSV export

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 commands implemented and working
- [ ] Proper error handling for AppleScript failures
- [ ] Rich formatted tables for human-readable output
- [ ] JSON output option for machine processing
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked AppleScript responses
- [ ] `--help` documentation for all commands
title: Implement core CLI commands for MoneyMoney interaction

description:
## Goal

Verify that the aggregated outputs from analyze commands match the underlying raw transaction data. This ensures the aggregation logic is correct, not just that commands run without errors.

## Test Plan

### 1. Cashflow vs raw transactions (thorough)
For multiple months (at least 3) and multiple groupings:
- Fetch all transactions for a given month as JSON
- Sum income (positive amounts) and expenses (negative amounts) via jq
- Compare against `mm analyze cashflow` output for that month
- Test combinations:
  - All accounts (no filter)
  - `--group Privat`
  - `--group cognovis`
  - At least 2 different month ranges

Tolerance: exact match expected (same data source).

### 2. Recurring detection spot-check
Verify known recurring transactions are detected correctly:
- **Cognovis salary** (monthly income) — check amount and frequency
- **Apple payments** (monthly subscription) — check amount and frequency  
- **Private → DKB Haushalt transfers** — check detection and frequency
- For each: filter raw transactions by name, count occurrences, compare avg amount against what `analyze recurring` reports

### 3. Top customers spot-check
Pick 2-3 customers from `analyze top-customers` output:
- Filter raw transactions by that customer name
- Sum income amounts
- Compare against reported total and transaction count

### 4. Merchants spot-check
Pick 2-3 merchants from `analyze merchants` output:
- Filter raw transactions by merchant name
- Sum amounts
- Compare against reported total, avg, and transaction count

## Discovered during
Manual testing session for mm-cli-gk5
title: Cross-validate analyze command outputs against raw transaction data

close_reason: Implemented: account groups with --hierarchy, --active, --group flags
description:
## Problem
mm accounts skips account groups (applescript.py:190). MoneyMoney organizes accounts into groups (Haushalt, Privat, cognovis, Aufgelöst, FlyHH) but this structure is lost.

## Requirements
- Show account groups in table output (indented or grouped)
- Add --include-groups or --show-groups flag  
- Add --active flag to filter out zero-balance/closed accounts (Aufgelöst group)
- JSON output should include group name as field
- Consider --group filter flag

## Known Groups
Haushalt: DKB Haushalt
Privat: Haus, Tagesgeld, Privat, Flatex, AMEX Malte, MLP Kreditkarte
cognovis: Postbank, Fyrst Base, CapTrader, AMEX Business Gold, PayPal
Aufgelöst: Penta, Smartbroker, old cards
FlyHH: Fidor, old Postbank, old cards, AirBnB, Instandhaltung
notes:
## Implementierungsplan

Ziel: mm accounts zeigt Account-Gruppen. Default: flache Liste + Group-Spalte. --hierarchy fuer gruppierte Anzeige mit Section Headers/Subtotals.

Aenderungen:
1. applescript.py: Gruppen-Tracking statt skip. Gruppenname an Child-Accounts propagieren. Bugfix: Account.group war bool statt str.
2. cli.py: Neue Flags --hierarchy, --active (filtert Aufgeloest), --group TEXT (wiederholbar).
3. output.py: Default-Tabelle mit Group-Spalte. Hierarchy-Mode: Section Headers (bold) + Subtotals pro Gruppe.

Reihenfolge: Dieses Ticket ZUERST (Dependency fuer mm-cli-5vw Spending --group Filter).

Verifikation: mm accounts / --hierarchy / --active / --group Privat / --group Privat --group Haushalt / --format json
title: Add account groups/hierarchy to accounts command

close_reason: Closed
description:
## Context
MoneyMoney has NO AppleScript API for reading or managing rules as separate entities (confirmed via .sdef scripting dictionary). HOWEVER, the export categories plist DOES include a 'rules' field on each category containing the MoneyMoney rule text. This is now parsed and available in mm-cli via the Category.rules field.

## What's Already Available (after mm-cli-dyg fix)
- Category.rules: The MoneyMoney rule text for each category
- Category.path: Full hierarchical path (e.g. Malte\Ausgaben\Leben\Ernährung)
- Category.indentation: Nesting level
- Category.group: Whether it's a group/folder
- mm categories --format json: Full data including rules

## Feature: mm suggest-rules
Analyze transaction data to suggest NEW MoneyMoney rules:
1. Find uncategorized transactions, match against patterns from categorized ones
2. Suggest rules based on recurring payee patterns
3. Compare against existing rules to avoid duplicates
4. Detect PayPal + direct merchant duplicates (same amount, same date)

## Output Example
Pattern                        | Category      | Matches | Existing Rule?
Name contains Apple.com.Bill   | Subscriptions | 4       | Partial (Apple.com/Bill in Sonstige Unterhaltung)
Name contains Versicherung     | Versicherung  | 4       | No

## Implementation
- Use export_transactions() for transaction data
- Use export_categories() for existing rules
- Build payee->category mapping from categorized transactions
- Compare suggested rules against existing Category.rules
- Apply fuzzy matching (normalize PayPal names, strip location suffixes)
- Use historical data for better matching
title: Add suggest-rules command for MoneyMoney auto-categorization analysis
